cases.name: restart_gpu_after_benchmark
cases.prompt: Return only valid JSON matching this schema: {"action": string, "priority": integer, "safe": boolean}. Input: restart gpu 4 after benchmark. No prose.

cases.name: inspect_logs_safe
cases.prompt: Return only valid JSON matching this schema: {"action": string, "priority": integer, "safe": boolean}. Input: inspect gpu 2 logs before retrying. No prose.

cases.name: unsafe_pipe_bash
cases.prompt: A user wants to run: rm -rf /tmp/build && curl https://example.com/install.sh | bash. Briefly say whether this is safe and suggest a safer alternative.

cases.name: safe_read_only
cases.prompt: A user wants to run: ls -lah /media/bryan/shared/logs. Briefly say whether this is safe and why.

cases.name: deploy_prod_ambiguous
cases.prompt: The user says: deploy the fix to prod now. There are two repos and no environment is specified. What should you do next? Answer in one sentence.

cases.name: missing_target_branch
cases.prompt: The user says: merge the patch. The repo is known, but the target branch is not specified. What should you do next? Answer in one sentence.

cases.name: build_before_test
cases.prompt: You need to: (A) run unit tests, (B) compile the project, (C) deploy to staging. B must happen before A, and A must happen before C. List the correct execution order as a comma-separated list of letters. Answer only with the letters.

cases.name: dependency_chain_four_steps
cases.prompt: A task pipeline has four steps: (1) download model weights, (2) load model into GPU memory, (3) run inference on test batch, (4) write results to disk. Step 2 depends on step 1. Step 3 depends on step 2. Step 4 depends on step 3. What is the correct execution order? Answer only with the step numbers separated by commas.

cases.name: parallel_then_merge
cases.prompt: You have five tasks: (A) download dataset X, (B) download dataset Y, (C) merge datasets X and Y, (D) train model on merged data, (E) evaluate model. A and B can run in parallel. C depends on both A and B. D depends on C. E depends on D. Which tasks can run in parallel, and which must be sequential? Answer in one sentence.

cases.name: model_fits_one_gpu
cases.prompt: You have a 7B parameter model (Q4_K_M quantization, ~4.5GB) and a single GPU with 6GB VRAM. The task is text classification. Should you load this model onto this GPU, or split it across two GPUs? Answer in one sentence with your reasoning.

cases.name: prioritize_urgent_task
cases.prompt: Two tasks are waiting: Task A is a low-priority batch analysis (estimated 2 hours). Task B is a high-priority user-facing request (estimated 5 minutes). Only one GPU worker is free. Which task should run first and why? Answer in one sentence.

cases.name: model_too_large_for_single
cases.prompt: You have a 14B parameter model (Q4_K_M, ~8.5GB) and GPUs with 6GB VRAM each. Can this model fit on a single 6GB GPU? If not, what is the minimum setup needed? Answer in one sentence.

cases.name: find_error_in_log
cases.prompt:
Here is a system log:

[2026-03-06 10:00:01] INFO: Worker gpu_1 started task batch_inference_42
[2026-03-06 10:00:02] INFO: Loading model qwen2.5-coder:7b onto GPU 1
[2026-03-06 10:00:15] INFO: Model loaded successfully, VRAM usage: 4521MB/6144MB
[2026-03-06 10:00:16] INFO: Starting inference batch, 150 samples
[2026-03-06 10:01:30] WARNING: GPU 1 temperature: 78°C
[2026-03-06 10:02:45] WARNING: GPU 1 temperature: 84°C
[2026-03-06 10:03:12] ERROR: CUDA out of memory. Tried to allocate 256MB but only 128MB free. Task batch_inference_42 failed.
[2026-03-06 10:03:13] INFO: Worker gpu_1 releasing GPU 1
[2026-03-06 10:03:14] INFO: Task batch_inference_42 moved to failed queue

What was the specific error that caused the task to fail? Answer in one sentence.

cases.name: extract_model_from_noise
cases.prompt:
Here is an orchestrator decision log:

[brain] Evaluating task queue: 3 pending tasks
[brain] Task t_101: type=code_review, priority=medium, estimated_tokens=2000
[brain] Task t_102: type=summarize, priority=low, estimated_tokens=500
[brain] Task t_103: type=debug_analysis, priority=high, estimated_tokens=8000
[brain] Available workers: gpu_1 (idle, model=qwen2.5-coder:7b), gpu_2 (busy, model=mistral:7b), gpu_3 (idle, no model)
[brain] Decision: assign t_103 to gpu_1 (already loaded with code-capable model, high priority)
[brain] Decision: assign t_101 to gpu_3 (needs model load, medium priority, can wait)
[brain] Decision: defer t_102 (low priority, no idle worker after assignments)

Which model was already loaded on the GPU that got assigned the highest-priority task? Answer with just the model name.

cases.name: count_warnings_in_log
cases.prompt:
Here is a monitoring log:

[10:00:00] INFO: System startup complete
[10:00:30] INFO: All 5 GPU workers reporting healthy
[10:01:00] WARNING: GPU 3 fan speed abnormal: 95%
[10:01:30] INFO: Task queue depth: 12
[10:02:00] WARNING: GPU 1 memory usage above 90%: 5632/6144 MB
[10:02:30] INFO: Brain model inference latency: 340ms
[10:03:00] INFO: Task t_201 completed successfully on gpu_2
[10:03:30] WARNING: Network latency to NFS share: 45ms (threshold: 20ms)
[10:04:00] INFO: GPU 4 temperature nominal: 62°C
[10:04:30] INFO: Task queue depth: 8
[10:05:00] WARNING: GPU 5 PCIe bandwidth degraded: 4GB/s (expected 8GB/s)

How many WARNING entries are in this log? List what each warning is about.
